title: Kitchens
citation_text: 1) Except as permitted by Sentence (4), a kitchen referred to in Sentence 3.8.5.2.(1) shall have a clear floor space not less than 1 500 mm in diameter for access to the sink and the range, regardless of the counters. (See Note A-3.8.5.7.(1).) 2) The bottom of the sink trap shall be located 230 mm above the floor. (See Note A-3.8.5.7.(2) and (3).) 3) The entrance of the sink trap shall be located a) not more than 330 mm from the wall behind the sink, or b) not less than 280 mm from the front of the sink. (See Note A-3.8.5.7.(2) and (3).) 4) A cooktop and a built-in oven are permitted to be used in lieu of the range referred to in Sentence (1), provided they are both served by the clear floor space referred to in Sentence (1).

title: Article 14
Article 14
Article 14
Article 14
Article 14
Article 27.1
citation_text: The roof structure of a building with a height of more than 3 floors and a height of more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units horizontally (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

title: Article 27.1
citation_text: The roof structure of a building with a height of more than 3 floors and a height of more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units horizontally (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.

title: Article 27.1

title: Article 14
citation_text: These Regulations are amended by adding after section 14 the following sections: "14.1. Despite section 14, natural second-day lighting of a room in a dwelling is permitted when the following conditions are met in addition to the requirements set out in subparagraphs (a) and (b) of paragraph 4 of section 9.7.2.3. of Division B of the Code: 1° second-day illuminated area and 1° second-day illuminated area with the glass surface providing natural lighting form parts combined within the meaning of the Code; 2° opening between the two areas shall be free from obstacles; 3° the glass surface providing natural lighting shall be at least 10% of the total surface of the combined rooms.

title: Article 23
citation_text: These Regulations are amended by adding, after section 25, the following subsection and sections: "SECTION VII SUSTAINABLE CONSTRUCTION" 25.1. The roof structure of an uncombustible building of more than 3 floors in building height and more than 250 m2 shall, when the roof slope is less than 2 units vertically in 12 units on the horizontal (2:12) or 16.7%, be designed to receive a type 1 green roof in addition to the loads prescribed by the Code.
